title: 10 best herbal capsules for swollen gums

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain antioxidants that reduce inflammation, which is a primary cause of gum swelling. The anti-inflammatory properties in sage also help to ease pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. Additionally, sage has antimicrobial properties that combat the bacteria responsible for gum infections, promoting healthy oral flora and reducing bleeding. By taking herbal sage capsules regularly, individuals can support overall gum health and alleviate symptoms of swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal sage capsules contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacterial infections causing gum inflammation and swelling, reducing the severity of symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines in the gums.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage capsules' antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of chronic conditions like periodontitis.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Salvia officinalis contains tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe irritated gums.

Rosmarinic acid (phenolic acid): Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic acid found in Salvia officinalis, has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat infections and inflammation in swollen gums.

Salvianolic acid (phenolic acid): Salvianolic acid, another phenolic acid present in Salvia officinalis, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect gums from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Salvia officinalis for swollen gums due to their high content of antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Stems: Stems are also used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in swollen gums.
Roots: Roots are used for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help combat infections causing swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning when the plant is at its highest moisture content level.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10 percent.
Weigh 10 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with 200 milligrams of the powdered salvia officinalis and seal them tightly.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain menthol, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and relieves pain. When taken orally, peppermint oil travels through the bloodstream and reaches the affected areas in the mouth, providing direct relief to inflamed tissues. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties help combat oral infections, further reducing inflammation and promoting healing of swollen gums. By targeting both the root causes of inflammation and its symptoms, peppermint capsules provide effective and natural relief for individuals suffering from swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal peppermint capsules contain menthol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Antimicrobial: Peppermint contains antimicrobial compounds like limonene and eucalyptol, which combat bacterial growth and prevent further infection of the gums, helping to reduce swelling.
Anesthetic: Menthol in peppermint capsules also has a numbing effect on the gum tissue, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort caused by swollen gums.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps reduce inflammation and ease pain by creating a cooling sensation on the affected area, thereby providing relief from swollen gums.
Menthone: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and swelling associated with gum inflammation.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and relief from swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have anti-inflammatory properties effective in reducing swelling and pain.
Stems: Stems are also used, as they contain some menthol and menthone, although in lower concentrations than the leaves, contributing to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Roots are occasionally used, but less frequently than leaves and stems, as they contain some menthol and menthone, which help to reduce swelling and pain in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Dry 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powdered mentha x piperita with 1 tablespoon of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mentha x piperita gelatin mixture using a small spoon.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. The herbal extract in licorice capsules has anti-inflammatory properties that target the underlying causes of gum swelling, such as irritation and infection. By reducing inflammation, licorice capsules can help alleviate symptoms like pain, redness, and sensitivity, promoting healthy gums and a comfortable mouth. Regular use of licorice capsules may even prevent gum swelling from recurring.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums.
Licoric acid: This component has antibacterial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to gum swelling.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against tissue damage caused by free radicals and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Licopyrocatechol glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate swollen gums by reducing the activity of inflammatory cells and enzymes in the affected area.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing in swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and soothes swollen gums.
Roots: Also contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help combat gum infections.
Roots: Possess a sweet, licorice flavor that helps mask unpleasant tastes associated with gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Extract 400 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra using 2 liters of 70% ethanol for 2 hours.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids.
Evaporate the liquid to obtain a concentrated extract using a rotary evaporator at 40°c for 2 hours.
Mix 500 milligrams of the concentrated extract with 1000 milligrams of starch and 100 milligrams of gelatin.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a gelatin capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds extracted from herbs such as chamomile, peppermint, and eucalyptus. When taken orally, these extracts travel to the affected areas of the mouth and work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the swollen gums, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing discomfort associated with gum swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Many herbal teas, such as turmeric and ginger, contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation.
Antimicrobial agents: Herbs like echinacea and goldenseal have antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to gum inflammation and infection.
Antioxidants: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants, such as green tea and peppermint, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate swollen gums.
remedy_constituents: Theaflavins: They help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in swollen gums, thereby alleviating symptoms.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with potent anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin helps to alleviate swelling, pain, and inflammation in the gums.
Catechins: Specifically, epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), a catechin present in Camellia sinensis, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help to soothe swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Buds: They contain flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective against swollen gums.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the gums, making them a useful part for capsule production.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality dried camellia sinensis leaves from a reputable supplier.
Weigh out 500 grams of the dried leaves and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or coffee grinder.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered camellia sinensis with 125 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the camellia sinensis gelatin mixture using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with swollen gums because of its powerful anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain. The herbal extract contains compounds like triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing inflammation in the gums. Additionally, Kansas coneflower has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling. By targeting both causes of swollen gums, Kansas coneflower capsules provide a natural and effective solution for promoting oral health and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower capsules contain compounds like cinnamic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the mouth, helping to soothe inflamed gums.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in kansas coneflower capsules, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the gums from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, reducing gum inflammation and promoting overall oral health.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas coneflower capsules have been found to modulate the immune system's response to periodontal infections, helping to reduce the severity of swollen gums and preventing further tissue damage.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and promote healing in swollen gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Caffeic Acid Derivatives (Cynarin, Chlorogenic Acid): These phenolic compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help control bacterial growth in the gums, reducing swelling and inflammation.

Alkylphenol Glycosides (Angustifolin, Cichoric Acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the gums, reducing swelling and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, although to a lesser extent, as they contain echinacoside, a compound that may help soothe and calm inflamed tissues.
Stems: The stems are used in some herbal remedies, as they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall after the first frost when the leaves have turned yellow.
Dry the harvested roots in a temperature-controlled environment at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered root with 2 grams of cellulose and 1 gram of silica gel.
Fill 1000 mg gelatin capsules with the root mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and pain. The capsules also have antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and fungi that contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, the antioxidants in pot marigold help protect the gum tissue from further damage. As a result, regular consumption of pot marigold capsules can provide relief from swollen gums, promoting healthy oral hygiene and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold capsules reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums due to their ability to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are chemical messengers that contribute to pain and inflammation.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold capsules help protect gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gum swelling and disease.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, and its capsules may help accelerate the repair of damaged gum tissue, reducing swelling and promoting a healthy mouth environment.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Flavonoids (including triterpenoids and phenolic acids): Flavonoids in Calendula have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and reduce swelling in the gums by neutralizing free radicals and modulating the immune response.

N-alkanoyl-4-hydroxyphenylalanine amide (HPP): HPP, a unique compound found in Calendula, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and promote healing in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate swollen gums.
Flowers: The flowers contain triterpenoids and saponins that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce gum inflammation.
Seeds: They are rich in calanolides and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and heal swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency and store them in airtight containers immediately.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 105°f for 8 hours or air drying for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder at a low speed for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered calendula with a small amount of filler material such as plant cellulose at a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

The herb's natural diuretic properties help to reduce excess fluid buildup in the gums, alleviating puffiness and discomfort. Anti-bacterial properties: The bitter compounds in dandelion have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, including those that can cause gum disease, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation.

Detoxification support: Dandelion is known for its ability to stimulate liver function and promote the excretion of toxins, which can help reduce the body's toxic burden and alleviate symptoms associated with swollen gums.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in swollen gums.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and reduce inflammation in the gums.
Taraxasterols: These triterpenoid saponins have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat infection and reduce swelling in the gums.

remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated and before heat of the day.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for eight hours.
Grind 2 grams of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Fill gelatin capsules with 200 milligrams of the taraxacum officinale powder using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight and children.

Grind the dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.

Dry the plant material in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin powder in a small bowl.
Encapsulate the mixture in empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey capsules helps with swollen gums because of its powerful anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain caused by gum inflammation. The herb's allantoin content also promotes healthy tissue repair and regeneration, helping to heal damaged gum tissue. Additionally, comfrey's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum inflammation. As a natural remedy, comfrey capsules provide a gentle and effective way to soothe and support oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey's allantoin content helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Wound-healing: Comfrey's rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Symphytum officinale, such as symphytine, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Iridoid Glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, including symphytin and symphytin II, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which help to alleviate swollen gums and prevent infection.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried symphytum officinale roots and stems from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5g of the powder with 5g of vegetable cellulose powder to create a uniform blend.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 capsules with the blended powder.
Label the capsules with the date and contents and store in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain antioxidants and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules work by reducing inflammation in the gum tissue, which helps to alleviate swelling and pain. Additionally, the antioxidants present in maidenhair tree capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum disease. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress.
Vasoactive compounds: The vasoactive compounds present in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help improve blood flow to the gum tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory agents: Ginkgo biloba contains anti-inflammatory agents like ginkgolides and bilobalide that can help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing saponins and flavonoids, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the gums.
Barks: The barks' phenolic compounds and tannins provide a soothing effect, reducing gum inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 3 hours or until crisp.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a mesh screen to remove lumps.
Mix 250mg of ginkgo biloba powder with 100mg of powdered slippery elm bark and fill gelatin capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months and take two capsules twice daily with water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. Antimicrobial properties: The herb has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation and swelling, such as periodontitis and gingivitis.
Wound-healing agents: Purple coneflower contains wound-healing agents like saponins, which promote tissue repair and accelerate the healing process of damaged or inflamed gum tissue.

Seeds: Contain lignans, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against the bacterial causes of gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried echinacea purpurea flowers and organic gelatin capsules of size zero for optimal potency and easy swallowing.
Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or electric spice grinder within 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered echinacea with 500 mg of organic gelatin in a small bowl and mix well to form a uniform blend for filling capsules within 15 minutes.
Fill empty size zero capsules with approximately one-half teaspoon of the echinacea-gelatin blend, making sure not to overfill or underfill each capsule within 20 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container made of glass or food-grade plastic and keep them away from direct sunlight and heat for up to six months without refrigeration.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried artemisia absinthium into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered artemisia absinthium with 100 milliliters of distilled water to create a tincture.

Grind 10g of dried vitex agnus castus berries into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200mg of the powdered berry mixture and seal them securely.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container, protected from light and moisture for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with swollen gums because of its powerful anti-inflammatory properties, specifically curcumin. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots and grind them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes.
Combine the turmeric powder with a filler agent like silica or calcium carbonate in a 20:80 ratio by weight.
Fill clean and dry gelatin capsules with approximately 300mg of the turmeric mixture and seal them immediately.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage periods.
Consume two capsules twice daily with warm water after meals for optimal relief from swollen gums symptoms within four weeks.

Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation, promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment. This natural relief from swollen gums can bring comfort and confidence back to those affected.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger capsules contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain in the gums.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to swollen gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and bone density.

Analgesic and antispasmodic effects: Herbal ginger capsules have analgesic properties that reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums, while their antispasmodic effects help relax the muscles in the gums and mouth, easing spasms and tension.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the underground stem of the ginger plant, which contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Roots: Ginger roots are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat various oral health issues, including swollen gums, by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from your local market or store them dried.
Clean and wash the harvested rhizomes thoroughly with water and a gentle soap.
Chop the cleaned rhizome into small pieces and then grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of ground zingiber officinale powder with 100 milligrams of lactose filler in a bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain mucilages, a type of soothing gel-like substance that coats and calms the gum tissue. When taken as a supplement, the mucilage from the marshmallow root works to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with swollen gums. By providing a protective barrier between the gums and irritants, such as plaque and bacteria, marshmallow capsules can help to promote healing and alleviate symptoms of gingivitis and periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which form a soothing film on the gum tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort by shielding the affected area from irritants.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by blocking pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antioxidant activity: Marshmallow root also exhibits antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, thereby promoting overall gum health and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm inflamed gum tissue.
Buds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis from a reputable supplier or your own garden.
Grind the dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground marshmallow root with 10g of glycerin and 50ml of distilled water in a small saucepan to create an herbal extract for 20 minutes.
Strain the herbal extract mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store it at room temperature for 2 days.
Fill #00 capsules with approximately 250mg of the herbal extract liquid and seal tightly to create the finished althaea officinalis capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet cicely capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain Myrrhis spiralis, a natural anti-inflammatory herb that soothes and calms irritated gum tissue. The capsule's active compounds work to reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and combat infection, promoting healthy gum function. Additionally, sweet cicely's antimicrobial properties help eliminate bacteria and other pathogens that can contribute to gum inflammation, leading to a reduction in symptoms such as redness, bleeding, and sensitivity.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sweet cicely capsules contain compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses, which can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling by reducing the presence of pathogens in the mouth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active ingredients in sweet cicely capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and hormones, helping to alleviate symptoms of swollen gums.

Wound-healing properties: Sweet cicely capsules contain antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and reducing oxidative stress, which can help to accelerate recovery from gum inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are underground stems that store starch, which is converted into a sweet-tasting sap when consumed. This sap has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections causing gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh myrrhis odorata leaves and stems from a trusted source or purchase dried herbs in bulk.
Dry the harvested herbs using shade drying or air circulation methods for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill each capsule with 250mg of the herbal mixture for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena capsules helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a compound called citral, which has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, lemon verbena's antimicrobial properties help combat the underlying bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria, lemon verbena capsules provide natural relief from swollen gums, promoting healthy gums and a fresh, clean mouth.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Lemon verbena's astringent compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in lemon verbena capsules help combat bacterial infections that can cause swollen gums, promoting healthy oral flora and preventing the spread of infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, soothing gum tissues and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the lemon verbena plant contain tannins that help to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce swelling and bleeding in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems for drying.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature.
Grind the dried verbena officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground verbena officinalis powder with gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:5.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hedge parsley capsules helps with swollen gums because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that directly target the source of the swelling. The capsules' bioactive flavonoids, particularly apiin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain associated with gum disease, while also promoting healthy blood flow and tissue repair. By addressing the root cause of swollen gums, hedge parsley capsules provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating discomfort and preventing further complications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Parsley's high antioxidant content can help reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress in the gums.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in parsley have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate swelling and discomfort in the gums.
Antimicrobial properties: Parsley has antibacterial and antifungal properties that may help prevent infections and reduce the risk of gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in gum tissues.
Barks: Contain tannins and flavonoids that have antimicrobial properties, which combat bacteria that cause gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest torilis japonica roots and dry them for at least 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 30 grams of dried torilis japonica roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 100 milliliters of boiling water to create a decoction that simmers for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids and leaving 50 milliliters of liquid.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-half teaspoon of the torilis japonica powder and seal tightly to store for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for swollen gums are a natural remedy that helps to reduce inflammation, pain, and discomfort associated with gum swelling. These capsules typically contain extracts from herbs such as tea tree, ginger, and turmeric, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. For example, the herb Calendula has been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the gums, while Echinacea has been known to boost the immune system, fighting off underlying infections that may be causing gum inflammation. By taking these herbal capsules, individuals can experience improved oral health, reduced pain and discomfort, and a renewed sense of confidence in their smile.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal capsules are various inflammatory and painful conditions. For example, herbal capsules may be used to alleviate symptoms of mouth sores, toothaches, and periodontitis. Other conditions like gingivitis, stomatitis, and oral thrush can also be treated with natural remedies in capsule form, providing a safe and alternative approach for managing discomfort and promoting oral health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with swollen gums is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Aloe Vera. Echinacea, an immune system booster, helps to fight off infections, while Ginger reduces inflammation and eases pain. Aloe Vera, rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, soothes and calms the gums. This combination works synergistically to alleviate swollen gums, promote healing, and prevent future occurrences. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements to ensure the best results.
